KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, To the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace.
text.Rotherham: Paul and Silvanus and Timothy—unto the assembly of Thessalonians in God our Father and Lord Jesus Christ,—favour unto you, and peace!
text.KJV: Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians which is in God the Father and in the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: I. Address Chapter 1 Greeting. Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy to the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: grace to you and peace.
text.DRB: Paul and Sylvanus and Timothee to the Church of the Thessalonians in God the Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ. Grace to you and peace.

text.NRSV: We always give thanks to God for all of you and mention you in our prayers, constantly
text.Rotherham: We are giving thanks unto God continually, concerning you all, making, mention, in our prayers,
text.KJV: We give thanks to God always for you all, making mention of you in our prayers;
text.NABRE: Thanksgiving for Their Faith. We give thanks to God always for all of you, remembering you in our prayers, unceasingly
text.DRB: We give thanks to God always for all you; making a memory of you in prayers without intermission,

text.NRSV: remembering before our God and Father your work of faith and labor of love and steadfastness of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Unceasingly, remembering—your work of faith and labour of love and endurance of hope, of our Lord Jesus Christ, before our God and Father:
text.KJV: Remembering without ceasing your work of faith, and labour of love, and patience of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, in the sight of God and our Father;
text.NABRE: calling to mind your work of faith and labor of love and endurance in hope of our Lord Jesus Christ, before our God and Father,
text.DRB: mindful of the work of your faith and labour, and of the charity, & of the enduring of the hope of our Lord Jesus Christ, before God and our Father:

text.NRSV: For we know, brothers and sisters beloved by God, that he has chosen you,
text.Rotherham: Knowing, brethren beloved by God, your election,—
text.KJV: Knowing, brethren beloved, your election of God.
text.NABRE: knowing, brothers loved by God, how you were chosen.
text.DRB: knowing, brethren beloved of God, your election:

text.NRSV: because our message of the gospel came to you not in word only, but also in power and in the Holy Spirit and with full conviction; just as you know what kind of persons we proved to be among you for your sake.
text.Rotherham: How that, our glad-message, came not unto you, in word only, but also in power, and in Holy Spirit, and in much assurance,—even as ye know what manner of men we became unto you, for your sake;
text.KJV: For our gospel came not unto you in word only, but also in power, and in the Holy Ghost, and in much assurance; as ye know what manner of men we were among you for your sake.
text.NABRE: For our gospel did not come to you in word alone, but also in power and in the holy Spirit and [with] much conviction. You know what sort of people we were [among] you for your sake.
text.DRB: that our Gospel hath not been to you in word only, but in power & the Holy Ghost, & in much fullness, as you know what manner of men we have been among you for your sakes.

text.NRSV: And you became imitators of us and of the Lord, for in spite of persecution you received the word with joy inspired by the Holy Spirit,
text.Rotherham: And, ye, became, imitators of us, and of the Lord, giving welcome unto the word, in much tribulation, with joy of Holy Spirit;
text.KJV: And ye became followers of us, and of the Lord, having received the word in much affliction, with joy of the Holy Ghost:
text.NABRE: And you became imitators of us and of the Lord, receiving the word in great affliction, with joy from the holy Spirit,
text.DRB: And you became followers of us, & of our Lord; receiving the word in much tribulation, with joy of the Holy Ghost:

text.NRSV: so that you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
text.Rotherham: So that ye became an ensample unto all who were coming to the faith, in Macedonia and in Achaia:
text.KJV: So that ye were ensamples to all that believe in Macedonia and Achaia.
text.NABRE: so that you became a model for all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
text.DRB: so that you were made a pattern to all that believe in Macedonia & in Achaia.

text.NRSV: For the word of the Lord has sounded forth from you not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but in every place your faith in God has become known, so that we have no need to speak about it.
text.Rotherham: From you, in fact, hath sounded forth the word of the Lord—not only in Macedonia and in Achaia, but, in every place, your faith which is toward God, hath gone forth, so that, no need, have we to be saying anything;
text.KJV: For from you sounded out the word of the Lord not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place your faith to God-ward is spread abroad; so that we need not to speak any thing.
text.NABRE: For from you the word of the Lord has sounded forth not only in Macedonia and [in] Achaia, but in every place your faith in God has gone forth, so that we have no need to say anything.
text.DRB: For from you was bruited the word of our Lord: not only in Macedonia and in Achaia, but also in every place, your faith which is to God-ward, is proceeded, so that it is not necessary for us to speak any thing.

text.NRSV: For the people of those regions report about us what kind of welcome we had among you, and how you turned to God from idols, to serve a living and true God,
text.Rotherham: For, they themselves, concerning us, do tell—what manner of entrance we had unto you, and how ye turned unto God from the idols—to be serving a living and true God,
text.KJV: For they themselves shew of us what manner of entering in we had unto you, and how ye turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God;
text.NABRE: For they themselves openly declare about us what sort of reception we had among you, and how you turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God
text.DRB: For they themselves report of us what manner of entering we had to you; and how you are turned to God from idols, to serve the living and true God,

text.NRSV: and to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead—Jesus, who rescues us from the wrath that is coming.
text.Rotherham: And awaiting his Son out of the heavens—whom he raised from among the dead,—Jesus: Who is to rescue us out of the anger that is coming.
text.KJV: And to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead, even Jesus, which delivered us from the wrath to come.
text.NABRE: and to await his Son from heaven, whom he raised from [the] dead, Jesus, who delivers us from the coming wrath.
text.DRB: and to expect his Son from Heaven (whom he raised up from the dead) Jesus, who hath delivered us from the wrath to come.

text.NRSV: You yourselves know, brothers and sisters, that our coming to you was not in vain,
text.Rotherham: For, yourselves, know, brethren, our entrance which was unto you—that it hath not proved void;
text.KJV: For yourselves, brethren, know our entrance in unto you, that it was not in vain:
text.NABRE: II. Previous Relations with the Thessalonians Chapter 2 Paul’s Ministry Among Them. For you yourselves know, brothers, that our reception among you was not without effect.
text.DRB: For yourselves know, brethren, our entrance unto you, that it was not vain:

text.NRSV: but though we had already suffered and been shamefully mistreated at Philippi, as you know, we had courage in our God to declare to you the gospel of God in spite of great opposition.
text.Rotherham: But, though we had previously suffered, and been insulted, even as ye know, in Philippi, we waxed bold in our God to speak unto you the glad-message of God with much conflict.
text.KJV: But even after that we had suffered before, and were shamefully entreated, as ye know, at Philippi, we were bold in our God to speak unto you the gospel of God with much contention.
text.NABRE: Rather, after we had suffered and been insolently treated, as you know, in Philippi, we drew courage through our God to speak to you the gospel of God with much struggle.
text.DRB: but having suffered before and been abused with contumelies (as you know) at Philippi, we had confidence in our God, to speak unto you the Gospel of God in much carefulness.

text.NRSV: For our appeal does not spring from deceit or impure motives or trickery,
text.Rotherham: For, our exhortation, is not of error, nor of uncleanness, nor in guile,
text.KJV: For our exhortation was not of deceit, nor of uncleanness, nor in guile:
text.NABRE: Our exhortation was not from delusion or impure motives, nor did it work through deception.
text.DRB: For our exhortation was not of error, nor of uncleanness, nor in deceit:

text.NRSV: but just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the message of the gospel, even so we speak, not to please mortals, but to please God who tests our hearts.
text.Rotherham: But, even as we have been approved by God, to be entrusted with the glad-message, so, we speak,—not as, unto men, giving pleasure, but unto God—who proveth our hearts.
text.KJV: But as we were allowed of God to be put in trust with the gospel, even so we speak; not as pleasing men, but God, which trieth our hearts.
text.NABRE: But as we were judged worthy by God to be entrusted with the gospel, that is how we speak, not as trying to please human beings, but rather God, who judges our hearts.
text.DRB: but as we were approved of God that the Gospel should be committed to us, so we speak: not as pleasing men, but God, who proveth our hearts.

text.NRSV: As you know and as God is our witness, we never came with words of flattery or with a pretext for greed;
text.Rotherham: For neither at any time were we found, using words of flattery,—even as ye know, nor a pretext for greed—God, is witness!
text.KJV: For neither at any time used we flattering words, as ye know, nor a cloke of covetousness; God is witness:
text.NABRE: Nor, indeed, did we ever appear with flattering speech, as you know, or with a pretext for greed—God is witness—
text.DRB: For neither have we been, at any time in the word of adulation, as you know; nor in occasion of avarice, God is witness:

text.NRSV: nor did we seek praise from mortals, whether from you or from others,
text.Rotherham: Nor, of men, seeking glory—either from you, or from others, though we could have assumed, dignity, as Apostles of Christ;
text.KJV: Nor of men sought we glory, neither of you, nor yet of others, when we might have been burdensome, as the apostles of Christ.
text.NABRE: nor did we seek praise from human beings, either from you or from others,
text.DRB: nor seeking glory of men, neither of you, nor of others.

text.NRSV: though we might have made demands as apostles of Christ. But we were gentle among you, like a nurse tenderly caring for her own children.
text.Rotherham: But we became gentle in your midst,—as though, a nursing mother, had been cherishing her own children:
text.KJV: But we were gentle among you, even as a nurse cherisheth her children:
text.NABRE: although we were able to impose our weight as apostles of Christ. Rather, we were gentle among you, as a nursing mother cares for her children.
text.DRB: Whereas we might have been a burden to you, as the Apostles of Christ; but we became children in the midst of you, as if a nurse should cherish her children:

text.NRSV: So deeply do we care for you that we are determined to share with you not only the gospel of God but also our own selves, because you have become very dear to us.
text.Rotherham: Thus, yearning after you, we could have been well-pleased to impart unto you—not only the glad-message of God, but, our own lives also,—because, very dear to us, had ye become.
text.KJV: So being affectionately desirous of you, we were willing to have imparted unto you, not the gospel of God only, but also our own souls, because ye were dear unto us.
text.NABRE: With such affection for you, we were determined to share with you not only the gospel of God, but our very selves as well, so dearly beloved had you become to us.
text.DRB: so having a desire to you, we would gladly deliver unto you not only the Gospel of God, but also our own souls because you are become most dear unto us.

text.NRSV: You remember our labor and toil, brothers and sisters; we worked night and day, so that we might not burden any of you while we proclaimed to you the gospel of God.
text.Rotherham: For ye remember, brethren, our toil and hardship: night and day, working, so as not to be a burden unto any of you, we proclaimed unto you the glad-message of God,
text.KJV: For ye remember, brethren, our labour and travail: for labouring night and day, because we would not be chargeable unto any of you, we preached unto you the gospel of God.
text.NABRE: You recall, brothers, our toil and drudgery. Working night and day in order not to burden any of you, we proclaimed to you the gospel of God.
text.DRB: For you are mindful, brethren, of our labour and toil. Day and night working, lest we should charge any of you, we preached among you the Gospel of God.

text.NRSV: You are witnesses, and God also, how pure, upright, and blameless our conduct was toward you believers.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are witnesses—God also, how kindly and righteously and blamelessly, unto you who were believing, we were found to behave;
text.KJV: Ye are witnesses, and God also, how holily and justly and unblameably we behaved ourselves among you that believe:
text.NABRE: You are witnesses, and so is God, how devoutly and justly and blamelessly we behaved toward you believers.
text.DRB: You are witnesses and God, how holily, and justly & without blame, we have been to you that did believe.

text.NRSV: As you know, we dealt with each one of you like a father with his children,
text.Rotherham: Even as ye know how, unto each one of you, we were as a father unto his own children, consoling you, and soothing, and calling to witness,—
text.KJV: As ye know how we exhorted and comforted and charged every one of you, as a father doth his children,
text.NABRE: As you know, we treated each one of you as a father treats his children,
text.DRB: As you know in what manner we desiring and comforting you, have adjured every one of you (as a father his children)

text.NRSV: urging and encouraging you and pleading that you lead a life worthy of God, who calls you into his own kingdom and glory.
text.Rotherham: To the end ye might be walking in a manner worthy of God, who is calling you unto his own kingdom and glory,
text.KJV: That ye would walk worthy of God, who hath called you unto his kingdom and glory.
text.NABRE: exhorting and encouraging you and insisting that you conduct yourselves as worthy of the God who calls you into his kingdom and glory.
text.DRB: that you would walk worthy of God, who hath called you into his Kingdom and glory.

text.NRSV: We also constantly give thanks to God for this, that when you received the word of God that you heard from us, you accepted it not as a human word but as what it really is, God’s word, which is at work in you believers.
text.Rotherham: And, for this cause, we, are also giving thanks unto God unceasingly, that, when ye received a spoken word from us—which was God’s, ye welcomed it—not as a human word, but, even as it truly is, a divine word,—which is also inwardly working itself in you who believe.
text.KJV: For this cause also thank we God without ceasing, because, when ye received the word of God which ye heard of us, ye received it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth, the word of God, which effectually worketh also in you that believe.
text.NABRE: Further Thanksgiving. And for this reason we too give thanks to God unceasingly, that, in receiving the word of God from hearing us, you received not a human word but, as it truly is, the word of God, which is now at work in you who believe.
text.DRB: Therefore we also give thanks to God without intermission: because that when you had received of us the word of the hearing of God, you received it not as the word of men, but (as it is indeed) the word of God, who worketh in you that have believed.

text.NRSV: For you, brothers and sisters, became imitators of the churches of God in Christ Jesus that are in Judea, for you suffered the same things from your own compatriots as they did from the Jews,
text.Rotherham: For, ye, became, imitators, brethren, of the assemblies of God which are in Judaea, in Christ Jesus, in that, the same things, ye, also suffered by your own fellow-countrymen, even as, they, also by the Jews:
text.KJV: For ye, brethren, became followers of the churches of God which in Judea are in Christ Jesus: for ye also have suffered like things of your own countrymen, even as they have of the Jews:
text.NABRE: For you, brothers, have become imitators of the churches of God that are in Judea in Christ Jesus. For you suffer the same things from your compatriots as they did from the Jews,
text.DRB: For you, brethren, are become followers of the Churches of God that be in Jewry, in Christ Jesus: for you also have suffered the same things of your own lineage, as they also of the Jews,

text.NRSV: who killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets, and drove us out; they displease God and oppose everyone
text.Rotherham: Who have both slain the, Lord, Jesus—and the prophets, and, us, have persecuted, and, unto God, are displeasing, and, unto all men, are contrary,—
text.KJV: Who both killed the Lord Jesus, and their own prophets, and have persecuted us; and they please not God, and are contrary to all men:
text.NABRE: who killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets and persecuted us; they do not please God, and are opposed to everyone,
text.DRB: who both killed our Lord Jesus, and the Prophets, and have persecuted us, and please not God, and are adversaries to all men,

text.NRSV: by hindering us from speaking to the Gentiles so that they may be saved. Thus they have constantly been filling up the measure of their sins; but God’s wrath has overtaken them at last.
text.Rotherham: Hindering us from speaking, unto the nations, that they might be saved, to the filling up of their own sins, continually; but anger hath overtaken them at length.
text.KJV: Forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they might be saved, to fill up their sins alway: for the wrath is come upon them to the uttermost.
text.NABRE: trying to prevent us from speaking to the Gentiles that they may be saved, thus constantly filling up the measure of their sins. But the wrath of God has finally begun to come upon them.
text.DRB: prohibiting us to speak to the Gentiles that they may be saved, to make up their sins always. For the wrath of God is come upon them even to the end.

text.NRSV: As for us, brothers and sisters, when, for a short time, we were made orphans by being separated from you—in person, not in heart—we longed with great eagerness to see you face to face.
text.Rotherham: Now, we, brethren, having been bereaved away from you, for the season of an hour,—in presence, not in heart, gave more abundant diligence, your face, to behold, with much longing;
text.KJV: But we, brethren, being taken from you for a short time in presence, not in heart, endeavoured the more abundantly to see your face with great desire.
text.NABRE: Paul’s Recent Travel Plans. Brothers, when we were bereft of you for a short time, in person, not in heart, we were all the more eager in our great desire to see you in person.
text.DRB: But we, brethren, deprived of you for a short time, in sight, not in heart; have hastened the more aboundantly to see your face with much desire.

text.NRSV: For we wanted to come to you—I, Paul, again and again—but Satan blocked our way.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, we desired to come unto you—even, I, Paul, both once and again,—and, Satan, thwarted us.
text.KJV: Wherefore we would have come unto you, even I Paul, once and again; but Satan hindered us.
text.NABRE: We decided to go to you—I, Paul, not only once but more than once—yet Satan thwarted us.
text.DRB: For we would have come to you, I Paul certes, once and again: but Satan hath hindered us.

text.NRSV: For what is our hope or joy or crown of boasting before our Lord Jesus at his coming? Is it not you?
text.Rotherham: For what shall be our hope, or joy, or crown of boasting? Shall not even, ye, before our Lord Jesus, in his Presence?
text.KJV: For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of rejoicing? Are not even ye in the presence of our Lord Jesus Christ at his coming?
text.NABRE: For what is our hope or joy or crown to boast of in the presence of our Lord Jesus at his coming if not you yourselves?
text.DRB: For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of glory? Are not you, before our Lord Jesus Christ in his coming?

text.NRSV: Yes, you are our glory and joy!
text.Rotherham: Ye, in fact, are our glory and joy.
text.KJV: For ye are our glory and joy.
text.NABRE: For you are our glory and joy.
text.DRB: For you are our glory and joy.

text.NRSV: Therefore when we could bear it no longer, we decided to be left alone in Athens;
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, no longer concealing our anxiety, we were well-pleased to be left in Athens, alone,
text.KJV: Wherefore when we could no longer forbear, we thought it good to be left at Athens alone;
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 That is why, when we could bear it no longer, we decided to remain alone in Athens
text.DRB: For the which cause forbearing no longer, it pleased us to remain at Athens, alone.

text.NRSV: and we sent Timothy, our brother and co-worker for God in proclaiming the gospel of Christ, to strengthen and encourage you for the sake of your faith,
text.Rotherham: And sent Timothy—our brother, and God’s minister in the glad-message of the Christ—that he might confirm and console you over your faith,
text.KJV: And sent Timotheus, our brother, and minister of God, and our fellowlabourer in the gospel of Christ, to establish you, and to comfort you concerning your faith:
text.NABRE: and sent Timothy, our brother and co-worker for God in the gospel of Christ, to strengthen and encourage you in your faith,
text.DRB: And we sent Timothee our brother, & the minister of God in the Gospel of Christ, to confirm you and exhort you for your faith,

text.NRSV: so that no one would be shaken by these persecutions. Indeed, you yourselves know that this is what we are destined for.
text.Rotherham: That, no one, might be shrinking back in these tribulations. For ye, yourselves, know, that, hereunto, are we appointed;
text.KJV: That no man should be moved by these afflictions: for yourselves know that we are appointed thereunto.
text.NABRE: so that no one be disturbed in these afflictions. For you yourselves know that we are destined for this.
text.DRB: that no man be moved in these tribulations: for yourselves know, that we are appointed to this.

text.NRSV: For when we were with you, we told you beforehand that we were to suffer persecution; so it turned out, as you know.
text.Rotherham: For, even when we were with you, we told you beforehand—we are destined to suffer tribulation! even as it also came to pass, and ye know.
text.KJV: For verily, when we were with you, we told you before that we should suffer tribulation; even as it came to pass, and ye know.
text.NABRE: For even when we were among you, we used to warn you in advance that we would undergo affliction, just as has happened, as you know.
text.DRB: For even when we were with you, we foretold you that we should suffer tribulations, as also it is come to pass, and you know.

text.NRSV: For this reason, when I could bear it no longer, I sent to find out about your faith; I was afraid that somehow the tempter had tempted you and that our labor had been in vain.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, I also, no longer concealing my anxiety, sent, that I might get to know your faith, lest by any means he that tempteth, should have tempted you, and, in vain, should have been our toil.
text.KJV: For this cause, when I could no longer forbear, I sent to know your faith, lest by some means the tempter have tempted you, and our labour be in vain.
text.NABRE: For this reason, when I too could bear it no longer, I sent to learn about your faith, for fear that somehow the tempter had put you to the test and our toil might come to nothing.
text.DRB: Therefore I also forbearing no longer, sent to know your faith: lest perhaps he that tempteth, hath tempted you, and our labour be made vain.

text.NRSV: But Timothy has just now come to us from you, and has brought us the good news of your faith and love. He has told us also that you always remember us kindly and long to see us, just as we long to see you.
text.Rotherham: But, when, just now, Timothy came unto us, from you, and brought us good tidings of your faith and love,—and that ye have good remembrance of us, continually, longing to see, us,—even as, we also, to see you,
text.KJV: But now when Timotheus came from you unto us, and brought us good tidings of your faith and charity, and that ye have good remembrance of us always, desiring greatly to see us, as we also to see you:
text.NABRE: But just now Timothy has returned to us from you, bringing us the good news of your faith and love, and that you always think kindly of us and long to see us as we long to see you.
text.DRB: But now Timothee coming unto us from you, and reporting to us your faith and charity, and that you have a good remembrance of us always, desiring to see us, as we also you:

text.NRSV: For this reason, brothers and sisters, during all our distress and persecution we have been encouraged about you through your faith;
text.Rotherham: For this cause, were we consoled, brethren, over you, in all our necessity and tribulation, through your faith;
text.KJV: Therefore, brethren, we were comforted over you in all our affliction and distress by your faith:
text.NABRE: Because of this, we have been reassured about you, brothers, in our every distress and affliction, through your faith.
text.DRB: therefore we are comforted, brethren, in you, in all our necessity, & tribulation, by your faith,

text.NRSV: for we now live, if you continue to stand firm in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Because, now, we live,—if only, ye, stand fast in the Lord.
text.KJV: For now we live, if ye stand fast in the Lord.
text.NABRE: For we now live, if you stand firm in the Lord.
text.DRB: because now we live, if you stand in our Lord.

text.NRSV: How can we thank God enough for you in return for all the joy that we feel before our God because of you?
text.Rotherham: For what thanksgiving can we render back, unto God, concerning you, on occasion of all the joy wherewith we rejoice, for your sakes, before our God?—
text.KJV: For what thanks can we render to God again for you, for all the joy wherewith we joy for your sakes before our God;
text.NABRE: Concluding Thanksgiving and Prayer. What thanksgiving, then, can we render to God for you, for all the joy we feel on your account before our God?
text.DRB: For what thanks-giving can we render to God for you, in all joy wherewith we rejoice for you before our God,

text.NRSV: Night and day we pray most earnestly that we may see you face to face and restore whatever is lacking in your faith.
text.Rotherham: Night and day, making very abundant entreaties, that we may see your face, and fit in the things which are lacking in your faith?
text.KJV: Night and day praying exceedingly that we might see your face, and might perfect that which is lacking in your faith?
text.NABRE: Night and day we pray beyond measure to see you in person and to remedy the deficiencies of your faith.
text.DRB: night and day more aboundantly praying that we may see your face, and may accomplish those things that want of your faith?

text.NRSV: Now may our God and Father himself and our Lord Jesus direct our way to you.
text.Rotherham: Now may, our God and Father himself and our Lord Jesus, make straight our way unto you:
text.KJV: Now God himself and our Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ, direct our way unto you.
text.NABRE: Now may God himself, our Father, and our Lord Jesus direct our way to you,
text.DRB: And God himself and our Father, & our Lord Jesus Christ direct our way to you.

text.NRSV: And may the Lord make you increase and abound in love for one another and for all, just as we abound in love for you.
text.Rotherham: And, you, may the Lord cause to abound and excel in your love one toward another, and toward all,—even as, we, do toward you:
text.KJV: And the Lord make you to increase and abound in love one toward another, and toward all men, even as we do toward you:
text.NABRE: and may the Lord make you increase and abound in love for one another and for all, just as we have for you,
text.DRB: And our Lord multiply you, & make your charity abound one to another, and toward all men: as we also in you,

text.NRSV: And may he so strengthen your hearts in holiness that you may be blameless before our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his saints.
text.Rotherham: To the end he may confirms your hearts, faultless in holiness, before our God and Father, in the Presence of our Lord Jesus with all his saints.
text.KJV: To the end he may stablish your hearts unblameable in holiness before God, even our Father, at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ with all his saints.
text.NABRE: so as to strengthen your hearts, to be blameless in holiness before our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his holy ones. [Amen.]
text.DRB: to confirm your hearts without blame, in holiness, before God and our Father, in the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ with all his Saints. Amen.

text.NRSV: Finally, brothers and sisters, we ask and urge you in the Lord Jesus that, as you learned from us how you ought to live and to please God (as, in fact, you are doing), you should do so more and more.
text.Rotherham: For the rest, brethren, we request and exhort you, in our Lord Jesus, [that], even as ye received from us, how ye must needs walk and please God,—even as ye also do walk, that ye would abound still more.
text.KJV: Furthermore then we beseech you, brethren, and exhort you by the Lord Jesus, that as ye have received of us how ye ought to walk and to please God, so ye would abound more and more.
text.NABRE: III. Specific Exhortations Chapter 4 General Exhortations. Finally, brothers, we earnestly ask and exhort you in the Lord Jesus that, as you received from us how you should conduct yourselves to please God—and as you are conducting yourselves—you do so even more.
text.DRB: For the rest therefore, brethren, we desire and beseech you in our Lord Jesus, that as you have received of us how you ought to walk, and to please God, as also you do walk, that you abound more.

text.NRSV: For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
text.Rotherham: For ye know what charges we gave you, through the Lord Jesus.
text.KJV: For ye know what commandments we gave you by the Lord Jesus.
text.NABRE: For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus. Holiness in Sexual Conduct.
text.DRB: For you know what precepts I have given to you by our Lord Jesus.

text.NRSV: For this is the will of God, your sanctification: that you abstain from fornication;
text.Rotherham: For, this, is a thing willed of God, your sanctification,—that ye should abstain from unchastity,
text.KJV: For this is the will of God, even your sanctification, that ye should abstain from fornication:
text.NABRE: This is the will of God, your holiness: that you refrain from immorality,
text.DRB: For this is the will of God, your sanctification: that you abstain from fornication,

text.NRSV: that each one of you know how to control your own body in holiness and honor,
text.Rotherham: That ye should know, each one of you, how, of his own vessel, to possess himself in sanctification and honour:
text.KJV: That every one of you should know how to possess his vessel in sanctification and honour;
text.NABRE: that each of you know how to acquire a wife for himself in holiness and honor,
text.DRB: that every one may know to possess his vessel in sanctification and honour:

text.NRSV: not with lustful passion, like the Gentiles who do not know God;
text.Rotherham: Not with a passion of coveting,—just as even the nations who know not God,—
text.KJV: Not in the lust of concupiscence, even as the Gentiles which know not God:
text.NABRE: not in lustful passion as do the Gentiles who do not know God;
text.DRB: not in the passion of lust, as also the Gentiles that know not God,

text.NRSV: that no one wrong or exploit a brother or sister in this matter, because the Lord is an avenger in all these things, just as we have already told you and solemnly warned you.
text.Rotherham: Not over-reaching and defrauding, in the matter, his brother; because, an avenger, is the Lord, concerning all these things,—even as we before told you, and solemnly called you to witness.
text.KJV: That no man go beyond and defraud his brother in any matter: because that the Lord is the avenger of all such, as we also have forewarned you and testified.
text.NABRE: not to take advantage of or exploit a brother in this matter, for the Lord is an avenger in all these things, as we told you before and solemnly affirmed.
text.DRB: and that no man over-go, nor circumvent his brother in business: because our Lord is revenger of all these things, as we have foretold you, and have testified.

text.NRSV: For God did not call us to impurity but in holiness.
text.Rotherham: For God did not call us, with a permission of impurity, but, in sanctification.
text.KJV: For God hath not called us unto uncleanness, but unto holiness.
text.NABRE: For God did not call us to impurity but to holiness.
text.DRB: For God hath not called us into uncleanness, but into sanctification.

text.NRSV: Therefore whoever rejects this rejects not human authority but God, who also gives his Holy Spirit to you.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, indeed, he that disregardeth—it is, not a man, he disregardeth, but God,—Who giveth his Holy Spirit unto you.
text.KJV: He therefore that despiseth, despiseth not man, but God, who hath also given unto us his holy Spirit.
text.NABRE: Therefore, whoever disregards this, disregards not a human being but God, who [also] gives his holy Spirit to you.
text.DRB: Therefore he that despiseth these things, despiseth not man but God, who also hath given his holy Spirit in us.

text.NRSV: Now concerning love of the brothers and sisters, you do not need to have anyone write to you, for you yourselves have been taught by God to love one another;
text.Rotherham: But, concerning brotherly love—no need, have ye, that we be writing unto you; for, ye yourselves, are, God-taught, to the loving of one another;—
text.KJV: But as touching brotherly love ye need not that I write unto you: for ye yourselves are taught of God to love one another.
text.NABRE: Mutual Charity. On the subject of mutual charity you have no need for anyone to write you, for you yourselves have been taught by God to love one another.
text.DRB: But concerning the charity of the Fraternity, we have no need to write to you: For yourselves have learned of God to love one another.

text.NRSV: and indeed you do love all the brothers and sisters throughout Macedonia. But we urge you, beloved, to do so more and more,
text.Rotherham: And, in fact, ye are doing it unto all the brethren [who are] in the whole of Macedonia; but we exhort you, brethren, to abound still more,
text.KJV: And indeed ye do it toward all the brethren which are in all Macedonia: but we beseech you, brethren, that ye increase more and more;
text.NABRE: Indeed, you do this for all the brothers throughout Macedonia. Nevertheless we urge you, brothers, to progress even more,
text.DRB: Yea and you do it toward all the brethren in all Macedonia. But we desire you, brethren, that you abound more:

text.NRSV: to aspire to live quietly, to mind your own affairs, and to work with your hands, as we directed you,
text.Rotherham: And to be ambitious to be quiet, and to be attending to your own affairs, and to be working with your hands,—even as, unto you, we gave charge—
text.KJV: And that ye study to be quiet, and to do your own business, and to work with your own hands, as we commanded you;
text.NABRE: and to aspire to live a tranquil life, to mind your own affairs, and to work with your [own] hands, as we instructed you,
text.DRB: and that you employ your endeavour to be quiet, and that you do your own business, and work with your own hands, as we have commanded you:

text.NRSV: so that you may behave properly toward outsiders and be dependent on no one.
text.Rotherham: That ye should walk reputably toward those without, and, of no one, have, need.
text.KJV: That ye may walk honestly toward them that are without, and that ye may have lack of nothing.
text.NABRE: that you may conduct yourselves properly toward outsiders and not depend on anyone.
text.DRB: and that you walk honestly toward them that are without; and need nothing of any man's.

text.NRSV: But we do not want you to be uninformed, brothers and sisters, about those who have died, so that you may not grieve as others do who have no hope.
text.Rotherham: But we do not wish you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning them who are falling asleep,—lest ye be sorrowing, even as the rest also, who are without hope;
text.KJV: But I would not have you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning them which are asleep, that ye sorrow not, even as others which have no hope.
text.NABRE: Hope for the Christian Dead. We do not want you to be unaware, brothers, about those who have fallen asleep, so that you may not grieve like the rest, who have no hope.
text.DRB: And we will not have you ignorant, brethren, concerning them that sleep, that you be not sorrowful, as also others that have no hope.

text.NRSV: For since we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so, through Jesus, God will bring with him those who have died.
text.Rotherham: For, if we believe that, Jesus, died, and rose again, so, also will, God, bring forth with him, them who have fallen asleep through Jesus;
text.KJV: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so them also which sleep in Jesus will God bring with him.
text.NABRE: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose, so too will God, through Jesus, bring with him those who have fallen asleep.
text.DRB: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, so also God them that have slept by Jesus will bring with him.

text.NRSV: For this we declare to you by the word of the Lord, that we who are alive, who are left until the coming of the Lord, will by no means precede those who have died.
text.Rotherham: For, this, unto you, do we say, by a word of the Lord,—that, we, the living who are left unto the Presence of the Lord, shall in nowise get before them who have fallen asleep;
text.KJV: For this we say unto you by the word of the Lord, that we which are alive and remain unto the coming of the Lord shall not prevent them which are asleep.
text.NABRE: Indeed, we tell you this, on the word of the Lord, that we who are alive, who are left until the coming of the Lord, will surely not precede those who have fallen asleep.
text.DRB: For this we say to you in the word of our Lord, that we which live, which are remaining in the advent of our Lord, shall not prevent them that have slept.

text.NRSV: For the Lord himself, with a cry of command, with the archangel’s call and with the sound of God’s trumpet, will descend from heaven, and the dead in Christ will rise first.
text.Rotherham: Because, the Lord himself, with a word of command, with a chief-messenger’s voice, and with a trumpet of God, shall descend from heaven,—and, the dead in Christ, shall rise, first,
text.KJV: For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God: and the dead in Christ shall rise first:
text.NABRE: For the Lord himself, with a word of command, with the voice of an archangel and with the trumpet of God, will come down from heaven, and the dead in Christ will rise first.
text.DRB: For our Lord himself in commandment, and in the voice of an Archangel, & in the trumpet of God will descend from heaven: and the dead that are in Christ, shall rise again first.

text.NRSV: Then we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up in the clouds together with them to meet the Lord in the air; and so we will be with the Lord forever.
text.Rotherham: After that, we, the living who are left, together with them, shall be caught away, in clouds, to meet the Lord in the air:—and, thus, evermore, with the Lord, shall we be!
text.KJV: Then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air: and so shall we ever be with the Lord.
text.NABRE: Then we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. Thus we shall always be with the Lord.
text.DRB: Then we that live, that are left, withal shall be taken up with them in the clouds to meet Christ, into the air, and so always we shall be with our Lord.

text.NRSV: Therefore encourage one another with these words.
text.Rotherham: So then, be consoling one another with these words.
text.KJV: Wherefore comfort one another with these words.
text.NABRE: Therefore, console one another with these words.
text.DRB: Therefore comfort ye one another in these words.

text.NRSV: Now concerning the times and the seasons, brothers and sisters, you do not need to have anything written to you.
text.Rotherham: But, concerning the times and the seasons, brethren,—ye have, no need, that, unto you, anything be written;
text.KJV: But of the times and the seasons, brethren, ye have no need that I write unto you.
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 Vigilance. Concerning times and seasons, brothers, you have no need for anything to be written to you.
text.DRB: And of the times and moments, brethren, you need not that we write to you.

text.NRSV: For you yourselves know very well that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
text.Rotherham: For, ye yourselves, perfectly well know—that, the day of the Lord, as a thief in the night, so, cometh;
text.KJV: For yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night.
text.NABRE: For you yourselves know very well that the day of the Lord will come like a thief at night.
text.DRB: For yourselves know perfectly that the day of our Lord shall so come, as a thief in the night.

text.NRSV: When they say, “There is peace and security,” then sudden destruction will come upon them, as labor pains come upon a pregnant woman, and there will be no escape!
text.Rotherham: As soon as they begin to say—Peace! and safety! then, suddenly, upon them, cometh destruction,—just as the birth-throe unto her that is with child,—and in nowise shall they escape.
text.KJV: For when they shall say, Peace and safety; then sudden destruction cometh upon them, as travail upon a woman with child; and they shall not escape.
text.NABRE: When people are saying, “Peace and security,” then sudden disaster comes upon them, like labor pains upon a pregnant woman, and they will not escape.
text.DRB: For when they shall say, peace & security; then shall sudden destruction come upon them, as the pains to her that is with child, and they shall not escape.

text.NRSV: But you, beloved, are not in darkness, for that day to surprise you like a thief;
text.Rotherham: But, ye, brethren, are not in darkness, that, the day, upon you, as upon thieves, should lay hold;
text.KJV: But ye, brethren, are not in darkness, that that day should overtake you as a thief.
text.NABRE: But you, brothers, are not in darkness, for that day to overtake you like a thief.
text.DRB: But you, brethren, are not in darkness; that the same day may as a thief over-take you.

text.NRSV: for you are all children of light and children of the day; we are not of the night or of darkness.
text.Rotherham: For, all ye, are, sons of light, and sons of day,—we are not of night, nor of darkness:
text.KJV: Ye are all the children of light, and the children of the day: we are not of the night, nor of darkness.
text.NABRE: For all of you are children of the light and children of the day. We are not of the night or of darkness.
text.DRB: For all you are the children of light, and children of the day: we are not of the night nor of darkness.

text.NRSV: So then let us not fall asleep as others do, but let us keep awake and be sober;
text.Rotherham: Hence, then, let us not be sleeping, as the rest, but let us watch and be sober:—
text.KJV: Therefore let us not sleep, as do others; but let us watch and be sober.
text.NABRE: Therefore, let us not sleep as the rest do, but let us stay alert and sober.
text.DRB: Therefore let us not sleep as also others: but let us watch & be sober.

text.NRSV: for those who sleep sleep at night, and those who are drunk get drunk at night.
text.Rotherham: For, they that sleep, by night, do sleep, and, they that drink, by night, do drink:—
text.KJV: For they that sleep sleep in the night; and they that be drunken are drunken in the night.
text.NABRE: Those who sleep go to sleep at night, and those who are drunk get drunk at night.
text.DRB: For they that sleep, sleep in the night; & they that be drunk, be drunk in the night.

text.NRSV: But since we belong to the day, let us be sober, and put on the breastplate of faith and love, and for a helmet the hope of salvation.
text.Rotherham: But, we, being of the day, let us be sober, putting on a breastplate of faith and love, and, for helmet, the hope of salvation.
text.KJV: But let us, who are of the day, be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love; and for an helmet, the hope of salvation.
text.NABRE: But since we are of the day, let us be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love and the helmet that is hope for salvation.
text.DRB: But we that are of the day, are sober, having on, the breast-plate of faith and charity, and a helmet, the hope of salvation.

text.NRSV: For God has destined us not for wrath but for obtaining salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
text.Rotherham: Because God did not appoint us unto anger, but unto acquiring salvation through our Lord Jesus [Christ]:—
text.KJV: For God hath not appointed us to wrath, but to obtain salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ,
text.NABRE: For God did not destine us for wrath, but to gain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
text.DRB: For God hath not appointed us unto wrath, but unto the purchasing of salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ,

text.NRSV: who died for us, so that whether we are awake or asleep we may live with him.
text.Rotherham: Who died for us, in order that, whether we be watching or sleeping, together with him, we should live.
text.KJV: Who died for us, that, whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with him.
text.NABRE: who died for us, so that whether we are awake or asleep we may live together with him.
text.DRB: who died for us: that whether we watch, or sleep, we may live together with him.

text.NRSV: Therefore encourage one another and build up each other, as indeed you are doing.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore be consoling one another, and building up, each the other,—even as ye are also doing.
text.KJV: Wherefore comfort yourselves together, and edify one another, even as also ye do.
text.NABRE: Therefore, encourage one another and build one another up, as indeed you do.
text.DRB: For the which cause comfort one another: and edify one another, as also you do.

text.NRSV: But we appeal to you, brothers and sisters, to respect those who labor among you, and have charge of you in the Lord and admonish you;
text.Rotherham: Now we request you, brethren,—to know them who are toiling among you, and presiding over you, in the Lord, and admonishing you;
text.KJV: And we beseech you, brethren, to know them which labour among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you;
text.NABRE: Church Order. We ask you, brothers, to respect those who are laboring among you and who are over you in the Lord and who admonish you,
text.DRB: And we beseech you, brethren, that you will know them that labour among you, and that govern you in our Lord and admonish you:

text.NRSV: esteem them very highly in love because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.
text.Rotherham: And to hold them in very high esteem, in love, their work’s sake. Be at peace among yourselves,
text.KJV: And to esteem them very highly in love for their work’s sake. And be at peace among yourselves.
text.NABRE: and to show esteem for them with special love on account of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.
text.DRB: that you have them more aboundantly in charity for their work. Have peace with them.

text.NRSV: And we urge you, beloved, to admonish the idlers, encourage the faint-hearted, help the weak, be patient with all of them.
text.Rotherham: But we exhort you, brethren—admonish the disorderly, soothe them of little soul, help the weak, be longsuffering towards all:
text.KJV: Now we exhort you, brethren, warn them that are unruly, comfort the feebleminded, support the weak, be patient toward all men.
text.NABRE: We urge you, brothers, admonish the idle, cheer the fainthearted, support the weak, be patient with all.
text.DRB: And we beseech you, brethren, admonish the unquiet, comfort the weak-minded, bear up the weak, be patient to all.

text.NRSV: See that none of you repays evil for evil, but always seek to do good to one another and to all.
text.Rotherham: See that none, evil for evil, unto any, do render: but, evermore, what is good, be pursuing, towards one another, and towards all:
text.KJV: See that none render evil for evil unto any man; but ever follow that which is good, both among yourselves, and to all men.
text.NABRE: See that no one returns evil for evil; rather, always seek what is good [both] for each other and for all.
text.DRB: See that none render evil for evil to any man: but always that which is good pursue towards each other, & towards all.

text.NRSV: Rejoice always,
text.Rotherham: Evermore, rejoice,
text.KJV: Rejoice evermore.
text.NABRE: Rejoice always.
text.DRB: Always rejoice.

text.NRSV: pray without ceasing,
text.Rotherham: Unceasingly, pray,
text.KJV: Pray without ceasing.
text.NABRE: Pray without ceasing.
text.DRB: Pray without intermission.

text.NRSV: give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
text.Rotherham: In everything, give thanks,—for, this, is a thing willed of God, in Christ Jesus, towards you:
text.KJV: In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you.
text.NABRE: In all circumstances give thanks, for this is the will of God for you in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: In all things give thanks. For this is the will of God in Christ Jesus in all you.

text.NRSV: Do not quench the Spirit.
text.Rotherham: The Spirit, do not quench,
text.KJV: Quench not the Spirit.
text.NABRE: Do not quench the Spirit.
text.DRB: The Spirit extinguish not.

text.NRSV: Do not despise the words of prophets,
text.Rotherham: Prophesyings, do not despise,
text.KJV: Despise not prophesyings.
text.NABRE: Do not despise prophetic utterances.
text.DRB: Prophecies despise not.

text.NRSV: but test everything; hold fast to what is good;
text.Rotherham: [But], all things, put to the proof—what is comely, hold ye fast:
text.KJV: Prove all things; hold fast that which is good.
text.NABRE: Test everything; retain what is good.
text.DRB: But prove all things: hold that which is good.

text.NRSV: abstain from every form of evil.
text.Rotherham: From every form of wickedness, abstain.
text.KJV: Abstain from all appearance of evil.
text.NABRE: Refrain from every kind of evil.
text.DRB: From all appearance of evil refrain yourselves.

text.NRSV: May the God of peace himself sanctify you entirely; and may your spirit and soul and body be kept sound and blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: But, the God of peace himself, hallow you completely, and, entire, might your spirit, and soul, and body,—[so as to be] unblameable in the Presence of our Lord Jesus Christ,—be preserved!
text.KJV: And the very God of peace sanctify you wholly; and I pray God your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: Concluding Prayer. May the God of peace himself make you perfectly holy and may you entirely, spirit, soul, and body, be preserved blameless for the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: And the God of peace himself sanctify you in all things: that your whole spirit, and soul and body without blame may be preserved in the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: The one who calls you is faithful, and he will do this.
text.Rotherham: Faithful, is he that is calling you,—who, also will perform.
text.KJV: Faithful is he that calleth you, who also will do it.
text.NABRE: The one who calls you is faithful, and he will also accomplish it.
text.DRB: He is faithful, that hath called you, who also will do it.

text.NRSV: Beloved, pray for us.
text.Rotherham: Brethren! be praying for us [also].
text.KJV: Brethren, pray for us.
text.NABRE: Brothers, pray for us [too].
text.DRB: Brethren pray for us.

text.NRSV: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a holy kiss.
text.Rotherham: Salute all the brethren with a holy kiss.
text.KJV: Greet all the brethren with an holy kiss.
text.NABRE: IV. Final Greeting Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.
text.DRB: Salute all the brethren in a holy kiss.

text.NRSV: I solemnly command you by the Lord that this letter be read to all of them.
text.Rotherham: I adjure you, by the Lord, that the letter be read unto all the brethren!
text.KJV: I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read unto all the holy brethren.
text.NABRE: I adjure you by the Lord that this letter be read to all the brothers.
text.DRB: I adjure you by our Lord that this epistle be read to all the holy brethren.

text.NRSV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
text.Rotherham: The favour of our Lord Jesus Christ, be with you.
text.KJV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen. The first epistle unto the Thessalonians was written from Athens.
text.NABRE: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
text.DRB: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
